title: Velvety Irish Eggplant Stew
description: A floral, velvety stew that brings the cozy warmth of an Irish kitchen to a low‑fat, vegetarian menu.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrot, and celery; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the diced eggplant, diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, oat milk, thyme, rosemary, and bay leaf.
Season with salt and pepper, bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low.
Cover and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the eggplant is tender and the flavors meld.
Remove the bay leaf, then use an immersion blender or transfer portions to a countertop blender to blend to a velvety consistency (blend in batches if necessary).
Return the stew to the pot, taste, adjust seasoning, and heat through for an additional 5 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of fresh parsley or a drizzle of low‑fat oat milk if desired.
